HeroSubtitle: Keep the mouse anchored to your main display while a touchscreen works on another monitor, with manual display mapping and fast return control.
StatusRunning: Protection is active. Touch input can work on the touchscreen display while your mouse anchor stays on the main screen.
StatusPaused: Protection is paused. Mouse and touch are currently using normal Windows behavior.
TrayRunningMessage: Touchscreen input is active while the mouse stays anchored to the main display.
TrayMinimizedMessage: Double-click the tray icon to reopen the dashboard.
OverviewHowBody:
1. Use the physical mouse on the main display.
2. Use touch on the touchscreen display for control interactions.
3. When you return to the mouse, the cursor snaps back to the last saved position on the main display.
OverviewWorkflowBody:
1. Place the physical mouse on the main display where you want to continue working.
2. Use the secondary touchscreen to tap, scroll lists, switch tracks, browse, or trigger control commands.
3. When touch work is done, move the physical mouse again to resume exactly from the last saved position on the main display.
4. If needed, adjust the screens in the Displays tab to ensure the correct monitor is marked as the touchscreen.
OverviewTipsBody:
Keep the main display for normal productivity and the touchscreen for quick control actions.
If the cursor does not return as expected, review the display mapping first.
Enable logging only during testing when you need to investigate unusual behavior.
DisplaysBody: Choose the monitor used as your normal mouse workspace and the monitor that should behave like the touchscreen control surface.
DisplaysTipsBody1: Use the readable labels to identify each screen by position and resolution. If the behavior feels off, first confirm the touchscreen display selection here before changing anything else.
DisplaysTipsBody2: Example labels: Main Display 1 (2560x1440 at 0,0), Left Display 3 (1920x1200 at -1920,0), Right Display 2 (1920x1080 at 2560,0).
BehaviorBody: Adjust how quickly MouseTool restores the cursor after touchscreen activity. Use Apply Changes after modifying any setting.
BehaviorRestoreNow: Restore the mouse immediately when touch is released
BehaviorRestoreHelp: Turn this on if you want the pointer to snap back instantly when touch ends. Leave it off to restore when the physical mouse moves again.
BehaviorAllowMouseTouchscreen: Allow the mouse to enter the touchscreen display area
BehaviorAllowMouseTouchscreenHelp: Enable this if you want the physical mouse to move freely into the configured touchscreen monitor. Leave it disabled to keep the mouse anchored to the main display workflow.
DiagnosticsBody: Use these controls when you want to capture behavior details during testing. Keep logging disabled during normal use if you want a quieter setup.
UpdatesBody: Check the published installer, validate it, and let MouseTool reinstall itself into the same location with your settings preserved.
UpdatesStatusUnsupportedBody: This workflow is available only in the installed build that includes the updater helper and Windows uninstaller.
UpdatesStatusCheckingBody: MouseTool is comparing your current version {0} with the published release manifest.
UpdatesStatusCurrentBody: No newer installer was found. Latest published version: {0}.
UpdatesStatusAvailableBody: A newer version {0} is available. Install it to close MouseTool, remove the current installed build, and reinstall the new one automatically.
UpdatesStatusDownloadingBody: MouseTool is downloading and validating installer version {0}.
UpdatesStatusApplyingBody: MouseTool is closing and handing off the reinstall flow for version {0}.
UpdatesStatusErrorBody: MouseTool could not complete the update flow. Details: {0}
UpdatesAvailableBalloonBody: Version {0} is ready to install from the dashboard.
UpdatesApplyingBalloonBody: MouseTool is restarting to install version {0}.
CloseDialogQuestion: How would you like to close MouseTool?
CloseDialogBodyRunning: MouseTool is currently protecting your workflow. You can keep it running in the tray, or stop protection and exit completely.
CloseDialogBodyPaused: MouseTool is currently paused. You can minimize it to the tray for quick access, or exit the app completely.
RestartRequiredMessage: The new language was saved. Please restart MouseTool to load the selected language.
BehaviorStartWithWindowsHelp: Enable this to launch MouseTool automatically when you sign in to Windows.
OverviewGratitudeMessage: Thank you very much for using this program, created with care by Marcos Vidal. If it helped you, feel free to buy me a coffee as a token of trust.
OverviewSupportNote: Help improve the project by sending suggestions, improvement ideas, or notes about your daily workflow.
OverviewHelpHint: Open the help file in the current language to review setup, usage, and quick troubleshooting.
OverviewCoffeeHint: If MouseTool improved your workflow, this button opens the support page for the project.
